jokes.text: I just wanted to remind you of the art show that's happening tonight at the Community Center. It's gonna be a lot of fun. - Great. Great. - Yeah. Well, that sounds cool. That sounds like something that'll be a big success, you know? I mean, like everything that you, uh--that you put your--your--your paws on-- your fingers.
jokes.setup: Ben tries to compliment Leslie's work
jokes.punchline: He accidentally says 'paws' instead of 'hands' and has to awkwardly correct himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Ben's nervousness around Leslie, with natural verbal fumbling that feels authentic

jokes.text: This isn't anything like your affair with Tom Haverford. - We weren't--you--you--
jokes.setup: Chris casually mentions Ben having an affair with Tom
jokes.punchline: Ben's sputtered reaction to this completely false assumption
jokes.explanation: Chris's oblivious nature creates absurd assumption, Ben's reaction is perfectly timed

jokes.text: Ooh, sparks are flying! I may have to call the fire department. That's a government joke.
jokes.setup: Chris makes an overly enthusiastic comment about sparks flying
jokes.punchline: He explains his own joke as a 'government joke' since they work in government
jokes.explanation: Classic Chris moment - overly literal and enthusiastic about everything, including explaining his own jokes

jokes.text: I'm like that lightbulb-- weak, flickering, barely giving off any light, unable to make out with the lightbulb I want to make out with.
jokes.setup: Leslie compares herself to a weak lightbulb
jokes.punchline: The metaphor takes an absurd turn to wanting to make out with another lightbulb
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor that starts logical then becomes wonderfully absurd, very Leslie-like mixing of professional language with personal desires

jokes.text: You know what might make you feel better? - A hug? - Paxil. Do you want me to get you a prescription?
jokes.setup: Leslie expects emotional comfort
jokes.punchline: Ann immediately suggests antidepressants instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with Ann's medical practicality trumping emotional support, great character consistency

jokes.text: How hot is the woman that's looking for a place to stay? - No, it's me. I'm looking. - Oh, come on, that's not fair. You shouldn't have led me to believe it was a beautiful woman.
jokes.setup: Tom assumes Ben is asking about a woman roommate
jokes.punchline: Tom's disappointed reaction when it's just Ben looking for housing
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom self-centeredness, predictable but well-executed character moment

jokes.text: I've been staying at the Pawnee Supersuites Motel for seven months. It's a charming little inn with wonderfully random wake-up calls, delightfully unstable temperatures, and, as of yesterday, bedbugs. 'Four stars!' Says nobody.
jokes.setup: Ben describes his motel stay sarcastically as 'charming'
jokes.punchline: The fake review quote 'Four stars!' Says nobody'
jokes.explanation: Well-structured escalating list with good punchline, though not groundbreaking

jokes.text: I wish I could help you out, Benihana, but I can't.
jokes.setup: Tom addressing Ben by name
jokes.punchline: He calls him 'Benihana' instead of Ben
jokes.explanation: Simple nickname wordplay, fits Tom's character but not particularly creative

jokes.text: I have a ritual, and it starts on the couch. - Ecch. - We sit down. - Okay. Yep. - Clap my hands. - Lights dim. - I understand. - Boyz II Men... - Please stop. - Fades in. - Nope. Nope.
jokes.setup: Tom explains his seduction ritual in detail
jokes.punchline: Ben's increasingly desperate attempts to stop Tom from continuing
jokes.explanation: Great escalating cringe with Tom's obliviousness vs Ben's horror, well-paced reveals

jokes.text: You can't use the front door. You have to climb in through the back window. No personal phone conversations. If you ever speak to me in Spanish, please use the formal usted. And no electricity after 6:00 P.M.
jokes.setup: April lists house rules for Ben
jokes.punchline: Each rule gets more absurd, ending with no electricity after 6 PM
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan delivery of increasingly ridiculous rules, the Spanish formality is especially unexpected

jokes.text: She's joking. Okay. You can use as much free electricity as you want. - It's free.
jokes.setup: Andy reassures Ben about the electricity rule
jokes.punchline: He emphasizes the electricity is 'free' as if that's the important part
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his childlike focus on the wrong details

jokes.text: if you ever watch a sad movie, you have to wear mascara so we can see whether or not you've been crying. There's no noise allowed on mondays and no TV after breakfast.
jokes.setup: More house rules from April
jokes.punchline: Mascara requirement to see crying, arbitrary day-specific noise ban
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd rules that feel authentically April - the mascara rule is particularly creative

jokes.text: We leave the tv on all day long so burglars think that we're home when we're not, which is my idea that I'm trying to patent.
jokes.setup: Andy explains their TV strategy
jokes.punchline: He thinks he can patent the common practice of leaving TV on for security
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy delusion about inventing something already commonplace

jokes.text: - You want my gum? - Mm-hmm.
jokes.setup: April casually offers her gum
jokes.punchline: Andy immediately accepts already-chewed gum without question
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment showing their gross intimacy

jokes.text: Okay, everyone, shut up! And look at me! Welcome to Visions of Nature. This room has several paintings in it. Some are big. Some are small. People did them, and they are here now.
jokes.setup: Ron reluctantly gives opening remarks
jokes.punchline: His aggressively minimal and obvious description of the art show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson speech - hostile, minimalist, stating only the most basic facts possible

jokes.text: I also think it's pointless for a human to paint scenes of nature when they could just go outside and stand in it.
jokes.setup: Ron continues his art show speech
jokes.punchline: His practical dismissal of landscape painting as pointless
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron logic - practical to the point of missing the point entirely

jokes.text: Anyway, please do not misinterpret the fact that I am talking right now as genuine interest in art and attempt to discuss it with me further. End of speech.
jokes.setup: Ron wraps up his speech
jokes.punchline: He explicitly warns people not to talk to him about art
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ron moment - proactively shutting down any follow-up conversation, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Are you guys frying marbles?
jokes.setup: Ben discovers something bizarre in the kitchen
jokes.punchline: The completely inexplicable activity of frying marbles
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Andy/April domestic chaos, the specificity of 'frying marbles' is wonderfully bizarre

jokes.text: We were checking to see if the fire alarm worked. It doesn't.
jokes.setup: Explanation for the marble frying
jokes.punchline: Their dangerous testing method failed and they learned their alarm doesn't work
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan delivery of their illogical but somehow reasonable (to them) testing method

jokes.text: The biggest challenge to picking the perfect roommate is finding someone who's willing to pay more than their share of rent without knowing it. I think we found that in Ben.
jokes.setup: April shares her roommate strategy in confessional
jokes.punchline: She admits to deliberately exploiting Ben financially
jokes.explanation: Dark humor perfectly suited to April's character - she's completely honest about her exploitation

jokes.text: So for my painting, I chose one of my very favorite Greek myths-- the centaur goddess Dyaphena slaying a great stag. - It's, uh, stunning. - It's breathtaking, Jerry. - Yeah. Really is. - Wow. Thanks, guys.
jokes.setup: Jerry describes his painting subject matter earnestly
jokes.punchline: Everyone's polite but clearly awkward reactions to what we'll learn is inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the reveal, the awkward politeness foreshadows the painting's inappropriateness

jokes.text: Oh. Oh, my God. That's me. Is that me? What? No. Oh, jeez, it does look like you.
jokes.setup: Leslie first sees Jerry's painting
jokes.punchline: Her shocked realization that she's the subject of the nude centaur painting
jokes.explanation: Great reveal moment with Leslie's horror and Jerry's weak denial creating perfect cringe

jokes.text: That's what you see when you close your eyes at night, Jerry-- topless Leslie glued to a horse.
jokes.setup: Tom commenting on Jerry's painting inspiration
jokes.punchline: His disturbing interpretation of Jerry's artistic process
jokes.explanation: Tom's crude but funny reduction of Jerry's artistic vision to something creepy

jokes.text: Besides ride an escalator and drive a car.
jokes.setup: Leslie praising what the centaur woman can do
jokes.punchline: Her practical observation about centaur limitations
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Leslie - taking the mythical seriously while noting practical concerns

jokes.text: Oh, my God. The baby is Tom. What? This is easily my favorite painting ever. What the hell, Jerry? Look at my potbelly. I look like a pregnant baby! And why am I so scared?
jokes.setup: Leslie notices Tom is also in the painting
jokes.punchline: Tom's horror at his depiction as a scared, pregnant baby
jokes.explanation: Great escalation as more characters discover their unflattering depictions, Tom's vanity makes this especially funny

jokes.text: In one brushstroke, Jerry has killed the Jay-Z vibe that's taken me years to cultivate and replaced it with a fat, brown, baby vibe, which is not as cool of a vibe.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining his image concerns in confessional
jokes.punchline: His clinical comparison of 'Jay-Z vibe' vs 'fat, brown, baby vibe'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom vanity and brand consciousness, the matter-of-fact delivery of 'not as cool of a vibe' is excellent

jokes.text: Uh, let's start with government-funded animal porn. Oh, I'm not sure that's fair. I want it destroyed, and I want a statement from this office apologizing for an obscene depiction of bestiality. Be--bestiality?
jokes.setup: Marcia's complaint about the painting
jokes.punchline: Chris's shocked repetition of 'bestiality' like he's never heard the word
jokes.explanation: Chris's innocent shock at the word itself is funny and in character

jokes.text: And how did it get like that? Who had sex with what and gave birth to which?
jokes.setup: Marcia questioning centaur biology
jokes.punchline: Her uncomfortably explicit questions about mythical creature reproduction
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy from the uptight character overthinking mythology

jokes.text: In college, I was in a nude production of Cats.
jokes.setup: Chris defending artistic nudity
jokes.punchline: His casual mention of being in nude Cats
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Chris moment - the mental image of nude Cats is disturbing and hilarious

jokes.text: Every great work of art contains a message. And the message of this painting is, 'get out of my way unless you want an arrow in your ass, Marcia.'
jokes.setup: Leslie defending the artistic merit
jokes.punchline: She interprets the painting's message as a direct threat to Marcia
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie moment - turning art criticism into personal combat, very quotable

jokes.text: Are you eating turkey chili off of a frisbee? It's pretty cute, right? No. Do you know what 'cute' means?
jokes.setup: Ben discovers April's unconventional dishware
jokes.punchline: April thinks eating off a frisbee is 'cute', Ben's exasperated response
jokes.explanation: Good domestic chaos comedy showing the April/Andy household dysfunction

jokes.text: We already spent your money on a new Xbox 360 and more frisbees to eat off of.
jokes.setup: Ben wants to leave but April says no
jokes.punchline: She reveals they already spent his money on Xbox and more frisbees
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of their financial exploitation plus callback to frisbee dishes

jokes.text: Cool. We'll get to be adults. Gee, golly, thanks, mister.
jokes.setup: Ben offers to teach them to be adults
jokes.punchline: April's sarcastic childlike response
jokes.explanation: Perfect April sarcasm delivered in fake innocent tone

jokes.text: Leslie, for our viewers at home who might not know, are centaurs real? - No. - You absolutely sure?
jokes.setup: Perd asks if centaurs are real
jokes.punchline: He follows up Leslie's 'no' with 'you absolutely sure?'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Perd moment - treating mythology like breaking news that needs verification

jokes.text: You guys--you wash your clothes in bubble bath? Eh, bubble bath, clothes soap, same thing. No, it's not. Well, they both make bubbles, so...
jokes.setup: Ben discovers they use bubble bath as detergent
jokes.punchline: Andy's childlike logic that anything that bubbles is the same
jokes.explanation: Good Andy childlike reasoning that makes sense to him but no one else

jokes.text: When'd you get in the business? - Last year. - Fantastic.
jokes.setup: Perd asks about Brandi's porn career
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic 'Fantastic' response to her timeline
jokes.explanation: Good Perd moment showing his inappropriate professional enthusiasm

jokes.text: What Leslie and I want people to know is you should be able to have sex anywhere you want and show it anywhere you want, whether it's girl-on-girl action, bondage, or what have you.
jokes.setup: Brandi tries to support Leslie
jokes.punchline: She completely misses the point and promotes public sex instead
jokes.explanation: Good cringe from well-meaning but totally off-base support

jokes.text: For me, it's when the penis goes in.
jokes.setup: Perd asks Brandi to define pornography
jokes.punchline: Her clinically simple definition
jokes.explanation: Perfectly deadpan, matter-of-fact delivery of crude content creates good contrast

jokes.text: Which I organize into a pile or stack that I put into the freezer. - Why? - So they won't get lost.
jokes.setup: Andy explains his bill organization system
jokes.punchline: He puts bills in the freezer so they won't get lost
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Andy logic - freezer preservation for paper documents

jokes.text: That's you in the painting. You're the fat baby! Aww... that's so cute. - Oh, you're into that? - No.
jokes.setup: Brandi recognizes Tom from the painting
jokes.punchline: Tom's hope that she finds his baby depiction attractive is immediately crushed
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection as Tom misinterprets her 'cute' comment as attraction

jokes.text: Imagine my horror. I'm hanging upside-down with my gravity boots watching Perd.
jokes.setup: Chris describing how he saw the TV interview
jokes.punchline: He was hanging upside-down in gravity boots while watching TV
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris absurdity - casually mentioning his bizarre fitness routine as context

jokes.text: Public art commission, filled with hippies who love public art and sometimes weed. Jackpot.
jokes.setup: Leslie confident about the commission decision
jokes.punchline: Her stereotypical assumptions about the commissioners
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie overconfidence based on broad stereotypes

jokes.text: If I had my laptop with me right now, I would show you a well-hidden folder with gigabytes of proof.
jokes.setup: Chris defending that he's not opposed to pornography
jokes.punchline: His overly detailed admission about his porn collection
jokes.explanation: Classic Chris oversharing - his honesty about porn is both inappropriate and in character

jokes.text: Please let the record reflect that the fat baby is referring to the painting. Hey! I am not a fat baby. I'm a small, slender man-- similar to actor Taye Diggs.
jokes.setup: Tom defending himself from the fat baby description
jokes.punchline: His comparison to Taye Diggs is completely delusional
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom vanity and delusion - Taye Diggs comparison is hilariously off-base

jokes.text: - The Romans... - Perverts. - The Greeks... - Gay perverts. To great societies in Europe. Europeans.
jokes.setup: Leslie lists great artistic societies
jokes.punchline: Marcia's increasingly simple dismissals ending with just 'Europeans'
jokes.explanation: Great escalating pattern with Marcia's prejudices getting lazier and more general

jokes.text: Yeah, well, there are nipples in it, so it just seems like we ought to be safe and destroy it.
jokes.setup: Commissioner explains their decision to destroy the painting
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of nipples as justification for destruction
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of Leslie's expectations about liberal art commissioners

jokes.text: Or we could buy a Wii. You can handle this. I don't know, man. Why don't you just do it? No, I can't. I--I...Have a date. Ooh, what's his name?
jokes.setup: Ben explains he can't shop because he has a date
jokes.punchline: Andy assumes Ben is gay and asks for the guy's name
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection joke showing Andy's assumptions

jokes.text: There's, like, a 30% chance they'll both die.
jokes.setup: Ben claims to have faith in Andy and April
jokes.punchline: His immediate honest assessment to camera of their survival chances
jokes.explanation: Perfect confessional moment - Ben's public confidence vs private honesty, great economy

jokes.text: Make me, stag! I am Dyaphena!
jokes.setup: Leslie steals the painting and runs away
jokes.punchline: She yells mythological references while escaping
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie moment - fully embracing the centaur goddess identity while committing art theft

jokes.text: Get it all. Grab everything that we've seen it on TV and put it in this cart right now.
jokes.setup: April finds As Seen on TV products
jokes.punchline: Andy's immediate decision to buy everything they've seen advertised
jokes.explanation: Good Andy impulsiveness and consumer gullibility

jokes.text: My heart is racing. It's going literally 45 beats a minute.
jokes.setup: Chris says he's feeling angry and his heart is racing
jokes.punchline: 45 beats per minute is actually very slow, not fast
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Chris moment - his ultra-health makes even anger result in an unusually low heart rate

jokes.text: But if you think for one second I'm not also gonna get that marshmallow shooter so that I can shoot you in the face with marshmallows when you're asleep, then you're the dumbest woman I know.
jokes.setup: Andy reassures April about buying adult things
jokes.punchline: His romantic promise to shoot her with marshmallows while she sleeps
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy/April relationship moment - combining romance with childish violence in their unique way

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Is that you as a naked horse? Does it look like me? I don't even-- Leslie, I mean, not to be inappropriate or anything, 'cause you're my boss and my friend, but I would totally hit that. - So would I.
jokes.setup: April and Andy see the centaur painting
jokes.punchline: They both casually admit they'd have sex with centaur Leslie
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy from their inappropriate but honest reactions to the painting

jokes.text: Oh, plates.
jokes.setup: Ben asks if they bought plates
jokes.punchline: April's delayed realization they forgot the most basic item
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective callback to their dish problems, April's delivery sells it

jokes.text: I mean, to be honest, that seems like the kind of thing I would do. And I didn't think you would give in.
jokes.setup: Ben sees Leslie giving up the painting
jokes.punchline: He admits this seems like something he'd do, not her
jokes.explanation: Good character insight showing Ben recognizes Leslie's usually stronger fighting spirit

jokes.text: She stole money from you. What? No, no, Andy, she did not.
jokes.setup: Andy tries to guess what's going on with Ben and Leslie
jokes.punchline: His first guess is that Leslie stole money
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy wrong guess showing his simple understanding of interpersonal problems

jokes.text: Look at me and April. If you're anything like us, three weeks from now, you'll be married, my friend.
jokes.setup: Andy gives Ben relationship advice
jokes.punchline: He uses his whirlwind marriage as a model for normal relationships
jokes.explanation: Good Andy obliviousness to how unusual his relationship timeline was

jokes.text: But I think you'll find no one could possibly be offended, unless, of course, they have a problem with centaurs having modern haircuts.
jokes.setup: Leslie reveals she had the painting altered
jokes.punchline: The only potential offense now is anachronistic hairstyles
jokes.explanation: Clever Leslie solution showing her ability to think around problems creatively

jokes.text: Is your penis between the front arms or the back legs? - Yeah, where's your penis? - Damn it, Jerry!
jokes.setup: People are asking Jerry about centaur anatomy
jokes.punchline: Multiple people asking about penis placement, Jerry's exasperation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy from inappropriate but logical questions about the mythology

jokes.text: And poundcake... am I right?
jokes.setup: Tom reassuring Jerry's wife about their relationship
jokes.punchline: He ends with a sexual innuendo about poundcake
jokes.explanation: Typical Tom inappropriate sexual comment, fits character but not particularly clever
